capabilities: Ensure task scheduling aligns with architectural goals by evaluating dependencies and priorities using defined metrics.

procedure.action: Capture task dependencies and priorities using a directed acyclic graph (DAG) representation using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Assert task dependencies with topological sorting to ensure no cycles exist by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field; assert field_validation_pass_rate >= 1.0.

Specify success metrics such as task completion time and resource utilization thresholds by specifying schema, constraints, and invariants in machine-readable form; include at least one falsifiable acceptance criterion where error_rate < 0.001.

Simulate task execution with a priority queue and measure against defined metrics using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.

Diff simulation results with expected outcomes to validate scheduling efficiency by computing diff(A, B) on each shared field; assert abs(A.value - B.value) < tolerance_threshold; report comparison_delta_count and max_delta_observed.

summary: Establish a robust evaluation framework for Dependency-Aware Priority Task Scheduling by integrating criteria, metrics, and validation steps from software architecture knowledge bases.
title: Dependency-Aware Priority Task Scheduling Evaluation

failureConditions: Tasks are executed out of order due to incorrect dependency resolution.
Resource bottlenecks occur due to improper priority assignment.

Metrics do not accurately reflect task scheduling efficiency; this occurs when the operation proceeds without pre-condition validation, allowing malformed input to propagate and corrupt downstream state silently.
metrics: task dependencies with topological sorting to ensure no cycles exist by asserting all required fields conform to the sch
successConditions: All tasks complete within defined time thresholds; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Resource utilization remains within acceptable limits during execution; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
Simulation results match expected task order and completion times; assert error_rate < 0.001 and operation_completed = true.
